idea: crible — self-hosted fundamental stock screener: worldwide universe (~161k equities) with Europe-depth priority, keyless open-data ingestion (FinanceDatabase, yfinance, ESEF XBRL filings, Stooq) into DuckDB/Parquet, 200+ ratios plus Piotroski/Altman/Beneish scores, filter DSL exposed via CLI, FastAPI and a React/Vite SPA, Docker-composed rolling prioritized crawler

product.name: crible
product.problem: Serious fundamental screening across ALL of Europe (and the world) is locked behind expensive feeds (EODHD €59.99/mo, FMP Ultimate $149/mo); free tools are US-centric (Finviz), shallow, or SaaS with no control over data, formulas or universe. Investors who want transparent, reproducible fundamental screens over European small/mid caps have no self-hosted option.
users: Individual fundamental investor (Maxime) screening European + worldwide equities with value/quality/forensic criteria
Technical tinkerer who self-hosts the stack, tunes the crawl priorities and writes custom screens via the DSL or SQL
product.valueProp: A screener you own: worldwide universe with Europe-depth, 100% functional with zero API keys (guaranteed forever), transparent formulas (financetoolkit + published score definitions), audited ESEF figures for EU names, DuckDB-fast filtering, and a single optional paid switch (EODHD) ready when deeper history is wanted — never required.

goals: Screen the full ~161k-equity worldwide universe on fundamentals in under 1 second locally (DuckDB over Parquet)
Europe as depth priority: EU companies enriched with audited ESEF XBRL figures and always crawled first
Zero-key guarantee: every core flow (ingest, compute, screen, UI) works with no API key configured, for life
Sustain a complete worldwide fundamentals sweep within Yahoo's free rate tolerance (~360 req/h, full cycle ≈ 19 days, quarterly freshness)
One-switch paid upgrade path: EODHD Fundamentals plugin specced and stubbed so paying €59.99/mo replaces the fragile yfinance link without touching the rest
nonGoals: Technical analysis, chart patterns or momentum signals
Portfolio management, backtesting or performance tracking
Multi-user SaaS: no accounts, no auth, single self-hosted operator
Buying any paid data plan in v1 (paid providers are specced as PRDs only)

constraints.budget: €0 for data in v1 (strict zero-key core; free-key plugins in phase 2; EODHD €59.99/mo is a documented future option, not a v1 dependency)
constraints.timeline: v1 zero-key end-to-end first; phase 2 (free-key plugins) after
constraints.deployment: Self-hosted via Docker Compose only (ingest worker + API/UI services, shared Parquet/DuckDB volume); repo private until v1 then public
compliance: Respect Yahoo rate tolerance (~360 req/h budget with jitter+backoff)
constraints.dataQuality: Every computed ratio/score traceable to source fields and a published formula; Beneish M-Score implemented in-house and tested against published examples

featureWishlist.title: Worldwide universe built from FinanceDatabase
featureWishlist.notes: Load the 160,995-equity universe (117 countries, 84 exchanges) into DuckDB; symbols are Yahoo-suffixed tickers usable directly by yfinance. market_cap is categorical (Large/Mid/Small) — numeric market cap enriched later from prices/shares. Region tagging drives crawl priority (Europe first).

featureWishlist.title: Rolling prioritized keyless ingestion (yfinance)
featureWishlist.notes: Continuous crawler under a global rate budget (<360 req/h, jitter + exponential backoff on 429, resumable). Priority queue: Europe → US large caps → rest of world; re-crawl driven by expected reporting freshness (quarterly). Per-ticker income/balance/cashflow (~4y annual, 4-5 quarters) + prices written to versioned raw Parquet. Providers are plugins behind one interface; with no key configured a keyed plugin disables itself cleanly.

featureWishlist.title: Ratio and score computation into a wide snapshot
featureWishlist.notes: financetoolkit (enforce_source=YahooFinance) for 150+ ratios + Piotroski F-Score + Altman Z-Score; Beneish M-Score implemented in-house (8 components, tested against published examples). Output: one row per company × period, ~200 columns, snapshot Parquet the screener queries.

featureWishlist.title: Filter DSL compiled to DuckDB SQL
featureWishlist.notes: Human query language: roe > 15 AND piotroski >= 7 AND country IN (FR, DE) AND sector = 'Industrials'. Compiled to SQL against the snapshot with a strict field whitelist (no injection), helpful errors, sorting and pagination. The 'ultra-powerful engine' is DuckDB: 161k × 200 columns in milliseconds.

featureWishlist.title: CLI (crible)
featureWishlist.notes: crible screen "<dsl>" (table or CSV out), crible ingest (run/resume crawler), crible status (universe coverage, data freshness, req/h), crible export. Same DSL as API/UI.

featureWishlist.title: HTTP API (FastAPI)
featureWishlist.notes: POST /screen (DSL or structured filters), GET /presets, GET /company/{symbol} (fundamentals history + scores breakdown), CSV export, GET /status (coverage, freshness). Serves the built SPA statically. No auth (self-hosted, single operator).

featureWishlist.title: React/Vite SPA
featureWishlist.notes: Results grid (TanStack Table): column picker, sort, filter bar bound to the DSL, presets menu, CSV export, company detail drawer (statements history, scores breakdown, data source + freshness badges). Dense data-first UI.

featureWishlist.title: Docker Compose deployment
featureWishlist.notes: Service ingest (continuous crawler) + service api (FastAPI + built SPA), shared Parquet/DuckDB volume, healthchecks, .env for optional phase-2 keys. docker compose up with zero keys gives a fully working system.

featureWishlist.title: Preset screens
featureWishlist.notes: Piotroski ≥ 7, Altman safe zone (Z > 2.99), Beneish red flags (M > -1.78), classic value (low EV/EBIT + high ROIC), quality (high ROE + low leverage). Each preset = a named DSL string, visible and editable — transparency is the product.

featureWishlist.title: ESEF XBRL Europe enrichment
featureWishlist.notes: Pull audited annual figures for EU issuers from filings.xbrl.org JSON-API (keyless, 4,000+ ESEF reports, xBRL-JSON). Reconcile/override yfinance fields where audited data exists; company detail links to the filing. Europe-depth differentiator.

featureWishlist.title: Stooq price fallback
featureWishlist.notes: Keyless CSV OHLCV for ~21k tickers as price backup when Yahoo rate-limits; keeps valuation ratios (P/E, EV) refreshable during Yahoo outages.

featureWishlist.title: Company detail view
featureWishlist.notes: Per-company page/drawer: statement history (as far as sources allow), every score with its component breakdown (e.g. the 9 Piotroski criteria), data provenance per field, freshness.

featureWishlist.title: Phase-2 free-key provider plugins
featureWishlist.notes: FinancialReports.eu MCP (EU filings + normalized financials, free account), SimFin bulk CSV (US, ~12-month delay), FMP free + EODHD free (schema validation only). Each optional: no key → plugin off, log line, no crash.

featureWishlist.title: EODHD paid provider PRD + stub plugin
featureWishlist.notes: docs/prds/eodhd.md: detailed PRD for the single planned paid source (Fundamentals Data Feed €59.99/mo — worldwide fundamentals, 100k calls/day), endpoint schemas validated with the existing free key, switch plan behind the provider interface. FMP Ultimate documented as the rejected alternative in a summary PRD.

nfrPriorities: reliability (rate-limit resilience: crawler never exceeds budget, backs off on 429, resumes cleanly after restart)
performance (any DSL screen over the full snapshot returns in <1s on a laptop; API p95 < 500ms)
portability (zero-key mode is a guaranteed contract; single docker compose up; no external services)
data quality & transparency (field-level provenance, published formulas, Beneish validated against published examples)
security (DSL cannot inject SQL — whitelist + parametrization; no secrets in images; keys via .env only)
maintainability (provider plugin interface; components isolated: universe/ingest/compute/store/api/ui; TDD with FR-tagged tests)
privacy (fully self-hosted; no telemetry; data stays on the operator's machine)

design.brandConstraints: Greenfield. Dense, data-first, terminal-inspired aesthetic; the table is the hero. Dark mode first-class.
design.tone: sober, precise, numbers-forward; French product name, English UI
